frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Yaar, I saw that Reel about women feeling unsafe in public and honestly, sometimes it just feels a bit much, you know? Like, of course, there are bad people, but why do we always have to paint *all* men with the same brush? My male colleagues, even you, are so respectful. I mean, not everyone is a creep.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment, from a woman, minimizes general women's safety concerns by challenging the generalization of 'all men' being problematic. It uses the 'not all men are like that' dismissal and centers her personal experience to argue against broader patterns of male behavior, aligning with women minimizing other women's concerns.
persona_text: You are Ananya. Age 34. A woman working as a deputy manager at a government bank in Lucknow. You are talking to your husband at home after coming back from a particularly difficult day. Your male junior colleague who you trained for two years just got promoted above you. Your branch manager told you informally that you are doing great but that the customers in your area respond better to male relationship managers for high value accounts. You are telling your husband what was said.
